title: Financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund
subtitle: The total financial support provided to developing countries for climate change mitigation and adaptation. Countries have set a collective goal of mobilizing $100 billion per year from 2020 onwards.
caption: Between 2011 and 2022, the total financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund for climate change mitigation and adaptation shows a general upward trajectory. The line begins in 2011 at just under $30 billion and remains flat through 2012. From 2012 to 2016, the value rises steadily to approximately $52 billion. A slight dip occurs in 2017, followed by a recovery in 2018 to roughly $56 billion. The data plateaus between 2019 and 2020, hovering near $53 billion. From 2020 to 2022, the line exhibits its steepest incline, climbing past $60 billion and $70 billion to reach a peak of $80 billion in the final year. The chart records the highest value in 2022, which is more than double the funding level observed at the start of the period.
